channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: El Niño Is Here and That’s Bad News for the Global Economy
item.description: The climate phenomenon arrives at a perilous time, with energy markets already strained from disruptions caused by the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui, Brian K Sullivan, Joe Wertz

item.title: Why the World Is Bracing for a Rare ‘Super’ El Niño
item.description: ​​A cyclical weather phenomenon that can drive flooding in one continent and drought in another has returned.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: El Niño Emerges in Pacific, Raising Heat and Crop Risks
item.description: El Niño has formed across the equatorial Pacific, setting the stage for months of droughts, floods and temperature fluctuations that will threaten communities worldwide along with agriculture and energy.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Solar Surpasses Coal in Historic Shift For US Electricity Mix
item.description: Solar overtook coal in US power generation in May, the first time the renewable source bested the fossil fuel in a calendar month.
item.dc:creator: Emily Forgash

item.title: Britain’s Garden Habits Are Making Their Homes Harder to Insure
item.description: As the UK moves from a record-hot May into an unseasonably wet June, the risk of flooding is being exacerbated by a proliferation of paved gardens, according to the country’s flood insurer of last resort.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Rudgard

item.title: Brookfield’s Neoen to Spend $7 Billion Doubling Australian Renewables Folio
item.description: French renewables developer Neoen SA expects to spend about A$10 billion ($7 billion) to more than double its Australian portfolio to 10 gigawatts by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Keira Wright

item.title: How Singapore Uses District Cooling to Beat the Heat
item.description: Its part of a S$100 billion climate adaptation plan.
item.dc:creator: Sing Yee Ong, Ishika Mookerjee, Todd Woody

item.title: Revego and H1 Mull Merger for $807 Million Africa Renewables Fund
item.description: Revego Fund Managers is exploring a merger with H1 Holdings that would create one of South Africa’s largest renewable energy-focused funds.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Changole

item.title: Extreme Heat Risks Losses for Indian Suppliers to Uniqlo, Tesco
item.description: Garment factories in India supplying customers like Fast Retailing Co.’s Uniqlo, Marks & Spencer Plc and Tesco Plc are suffering productivity losses of as much as 10% as a result of the nation’s extreme heat, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Azman Usmani

item.title: Singapore Brings Back 19th-Century Tech to Beat Warming Climate
item.description: Deep underneath Singapore’s northeastern district of Punggol, a five-kilometer network of metal pipes roars as it pumps chilled water to cool offices and classrooms overhead.
item.dc:creator: Sing Yee Ong, Ishika Mookerjee
